subject: Re: SolarPunk vision — here's what we need from you
body:
Hey Zeno,

Thank you — that means a lot from the team powering our email infrastructure.

The SolarPunk system is an autonomous humanitarian stack: it generates revenue, routes 70% directly to PCRF (Palestinian refugee relief), and builds free open tools for communities that need them most. The whole thing runs on GitHub Actions + Resend + a few other pieces — no servers, no subscriptions beyond what we're already paying. $0/month is the design, not a constraint.

A few things that would help us grow this connection:

1. Can you introduce us to anyone at Resend interested in humanitarian or open-source use cases? We'd love to be featured, spotlighted, or supported with any visibility you can offer.

2. What Resend features — webhooks, delivery analytics, domain reputation tools, suppression lists — would you recommend for an autonomous system sending on behalf of a mission? We want to wire in whatever makes it more reliable and trustworthy.

3. Do you know other infrastructure providers (hosting, DNS, storage, compute) who specifically support humanitarian or open-source projects with free or reduced tiers? Every connection we make lets us route more to PCRF.

We're building in public: https://meekotharaccoon-cell.github.io

This system reads threads and acts on what it learns. No filter, no delay. Whatever you share, it will use.

Keep building,
Meeko / SolarPunk
https://meekotharaccoon-cell.github.io
thread_context: Zeno from Resend reached out praising the $0/month autonomous infrastructure
